titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/210

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=475 status=live modified=2021-04-06 16:02:26.968678+00 | translation moa=476 status=live modified=2021-04-06 16:02:24.555125+00

div.p:
Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Iohannes filius Michaelis de Methkil . Salutem . Nouerit vniuersitas
uestra me dedisse concessisse et Hac Carta me confirmasse Deo et Ecclesie Sancte Marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem
Deo seruientibus et seruituris totam terram meam de Panneschelys . Tenendam et habendam eisdem Monachis de me
et heredibus meis in puram et perpetuam Elemosinam in perpetuum . Ita Libere . quiete . plenarie . et honorifice  sicut
aliqua Elemosina in Regno Scocie . Liberius . quietius . et plenius . Tenetur vel possidetur . In Bosco et
Plano . In terris cultis et incultis . In pratis . et Pascuis . In viis et Semitis . In Moris . Mosis . et Mare
siis . In aquis . et stagnis . Viuariis . et Molendinis . et omnibus aliis asiamentis que infra predictam terram
inueniri poterunt aut excerceri . Ego autem et heredes mei manutebimus et defendemus predictis Monachis
totam predictam terram cum omnibus Libertatibus et iustis pertinentiis suis contra omnes homines . Excepta terra quam dedi Monachis de May . et excepta terra quam concesse Henrico paruo et heredibus suis per rectas diuisas suas in territorio de Panneschelys . Totam uero terram predictam sicut dictum est Tenebunt
et habebunt prefati Monachi de Melros Liberam et quietam ab omni seruitio consuetudine et exactione
et demanda de me et heredibus meis in perpetuum . Hiis Testibus . Thoma Comite de Æthol’ . Thomas
de Cantia . Magistro Iohanne abigensi . Magistro nessio medice domini Regis . Gaufrido de Bosco . Iacobo
filio Willelmi . Andrea de Suintun’ . et Multis aliis .

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and to come John son of Michael of Methil sends greeting.

Let your whole community know me to have given, granted, and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place now and to come, my whole land at Penshiel; for the same monks to hold and to have of me and my heirs in pure and perpetual alms for ever; as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is freely, quitely, and fully held and possessed; in wood and plain, in lands cultivated and uncultivated, in meadows and pastures, in roads and paths, in moors, peat-bogs and marshes, in running waters and ponds, fish-ponds and mills, and all other easments that can be found or employed within the said land. And I and my heirs shall maintain and defend to the said monks the whole aforesaid land with all its liberties and just pertinents against all men; except for the land that I gave to the monks of May, and except for the land that I allowed to Henry Little and his heirs by its correct boundaries in the territory of Penshiels. The whole aforesaid land, as it is said, the aforementioned monks shall hold and have free and quit from every service, custom and exaction and demand of me and my heirs for ever.
